description: Refactor the decision process builder navigation from a two-level model (horizontal step tabs + vertical section tabs) into a single unified left sidebar with tree-style menu items

userStories.title: Flatten navigation config and content registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to refactor the navigation configuration from a two-level (step → section) model to a flat list of sections so the sidebar can directly select any content section.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a flat SIDEBAR_ITEMS array in navigationConfig.ts that lists all sections in display order: overview, phases, proposalCategories, templateEditor, roles, participants
Each item has: id (SectionId), labelKey (TranslationKey), and optional parentStepId (StepId) for content registry lookup
Update contentRegistry.tsx to export a getContentComponentFlat(sectionId) function that maps section IDs directly to components without requiring stepId
Keep existing STEPS, SECTIONS_BY_STEP, and getContentComponent for backward compatibility during migration
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update useProcessNavigation hook for flat model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the navigation hook to use a single section query parameter instead of step + section so the flat sidebar can drive navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: useProcessNavigation uses a single 'section' query parameter instead of 'step' + 'section'
All sections are addressable directly (e.g., ?section=overview, ?section=phases, ?section=proposalCategories, ?section=templateEditor, ?section=roles, ?section=participants)
Hook returns currentSection, visibleSections, and setSection (no more step-level state)
Backward compatibility: if old ?step=general&section=overview params are present, derive the correct section and redirect
ProcessBuilderContent.tsx updated to use the flat content lookup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create unified sidebar navigation component
userStories.description: As a process builder user, I want all navigation items in a single left sidebar so I can see the full process structure at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor ProcessBuilderSectionNav.tsx (ProcessBuilderSidebar) to render all navigation items from the flat SIDEBAR_ITEMS config
Top-level items displayed: Overview, Phases, Proposal Categories, Proposal Template, Roles, Participants
Active item highlighted with teal text and light teal background (bg-primary-teal96White / text-primary)
Sidebar is 240px wide on desktop with a right border separator (border-r)
Items are rendered as clickable buttons/tabs that call setSection from the updated hook
Sidebar is sticky and scrollable if items overflow
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add dynamic phase sub-items under Phases in sidebar
userStories.description: As a process builder user, I want to see the configured phases listed under Phases in the sidebar so I can navigate directly to a specific phase.
acceptanceCriteria: Phase sub-items appear indented below the Phases item in the sidebar
Sub-items use a LuCornerDownRight icon from react-icons/lu with smaller text (text-sm / 12px) compared to top-level items (text-base / 14px)
Phase names are pulled dynamically from the process builder store or tRPC query
Clicking a phase sub-item navigates to the phases section (since per-phase config views don't exist yet, navigate to the Phases section)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Simplify header to breadcrumbs only
userStories.description: As a process builder user, I want a clean minimal header showing only breadcrumbs and user avatar so the sidebar handles all process navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Header shows only: Home link with house icon, chevron separator, process name, and user avatar on the right
Horizontal step tabs are removed from the header entirely
Launch/Save button is removed from the header (will be in bottom bar)
Validation steps-remaining popover is removed from the header (will be in bottom bar)
Header is a single row at ~56px height
SidebarProvider wrapping is preserved for mobile trigger functionality
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create bottom action bar with Launch/Save button
userStories.description: As a process builder user, I want the Launch Process / Save button in a sticky bottom bar alongside Exit and Back so all actions are consistently at the bottom.
acceptanceCriteria: New ProcessBuilderFooter component renders as a sticky footer bar with top border and backdrop blur
Exit button with rotated LuLogOut icon on the bottom-left navigates back to decision list or home
Back button next to Exit (secondary style)
Launch Process / Update Process button on the bottom-right (primary teal style)
Validation steps-remaining indicator appears next to the Launch button
Footer is added to the edit page layout below the sidebar+content area
The existing progress bar / stepper in the center is kept as-is (no logic changes)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update mobile navigation to use unified sidebar
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the sidebar to be accessible via a hamburger trigger so I can navigate on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile, sidebar is hidden by default and toggled via the existing SidebarTrigger in the header
Mobile sidebar shows the same unified navigation items as the desktop sidebar
Selecting a sidebar item on mobile closes the sidebar drawer and navigates to the section
The MobileSidebar component in ProcessBuilderHeader is updated to use the flat navigation items instead of step tabs
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add translation keys for all new sidebar strings
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to ensure all new user-facing strings are properly internationalized across all language files.
acceptanceCriteria: All sidebar labels use t('...') from useTranslations hook
New translation keys (Exit, Back, any new labels) are added to ALL .json files in apps/app/src/lib/i18n/dictionaries/
No hardcoded user-facing strings remain in ProcessBuilderSidebar, ProcessBuilderHeader, or ProcessBuilderFooter
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All translation keys were already added in prior stories (US-003, US-005, US-006, US-007). Verified all keys present in all 5 dictionary files.
